worth_it_explanation: Targeting on LinkedIn is built from professional attributes members keep in their profiles, so the way an audience is assembled decides who sees the ad and how steadily it delivers. These checks are quick to run inside Campaign Manager. The audience size estimate sits right in the setup, the targeting facets are listed in the audience panel, and the Audience Expansion checkbox is one click to read. Each fix is a configuration change to the targeting rather than a rebuild of the campaign, and it takes effect the next time the audience refreshes.
intro: LinkedIn audiences are assembled from professional attributes members enter on their profiles, such as job title, seniority, job function, skills, and company. The setup also reports an estimated audience size and carries an Audience Expansion setting that can widen delivery past the attributes the advertiser selected. These checks confirm that each audience clears LinkedIn's recommended size floor, that attribute layering is kept durable rather than over-stacked, and that Audience Expansion is set the way the advertiser intends.

audit_checks.title: Audiences are below the recommended size floor
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An audience built below LinkedIn's recommended floor gives the delivery system too few members to pace against, so impressions arrive in bursts and cost per result climbs. The campaign can still spend, but it spends inefficiently against a thin pool.

educate.base:
LinkedIn reports an estimated audience size as targeting is assembled in Campaign Manager, and it publishes guidance on how large that audience should be for steady delivery. The absolute minimum to run a campaign is 300 members, but for Sponsored Content and Sponsored Messaging LinkedIn suggests a minimum of 300,000, per its target audience size best practices.

When an audience sits well below that floor, the delivery system has a small pool to draw from. It cannot pace impressions evenly across the flight, so spend arrives in bursts, the bid has to climb to win the few available auctions, and cost per result rises. A thin audience also makes results noisy since a handful of members can swing the reported numbers.

The size estimate is visible during setup, so you may want to confirm each active audience against the recommended floor for its ad format. A few signs point to an audience that is too narrow:
  • The estimated audience size shown in setup is in the low tens of thousands or smaller for Sponsored Content
  • The campaign delivers in uneven bursts rather than pacing across the day
  • Cost per result is high relative to comparable campaigns on broader audiences
You may want to consider broadening the targeting, starting wider and narrowing later using demographic reporting, or consolidating several small audiences that share an objective.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business (B2B) store selling higher-priced or subscription products, the buying pool on LinkedIn can be genuinely small, so an audience below the floor may reflect the real market rather than a setup error. You may want to confirm whether the narrow size comes from over-targeting or from a market that is simply limited, and broaden the attribute targeting before assuming the audience is mis-built.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead generation is the dominant case on LinkedIn, and a narrow audience here throttles the form-fill volume the campaign can produce. You may want to confirm the audience clears the recommended floor for its ad format so the delivery system can pace lead volume steadily rather than in bursts.

investigate.intro: Confirm the estimated audience size for each active campaign against LinkedIn's recommended floor for its ad format.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager and select the advertising account, then open 'Campaigns' and choose a campaign you want to confirm.
Open the campaign's audience setup and read the 'Forecasted results' or audience size estimate panel, which reports the estimated number of members the targeting reaches.
Note the estimate against the recommended floor for the ad format, for example 300,000 for Sponsored Content, and record any audience that sits well below it.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active campaign, the estimated audience size from setup and the recommended floor for its ad format. A campaign whose audience clears the recommended floor passes. A campaign whose audience sits well below the floor is a fail recorded with the campaign name and the estimated size.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the estimated audience size and ad format for each active campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active audiences on {{site_name}} sit below LinkedIn's recommended size floor for their ad format. A pool this thin gives the delivery system too few members to pace against, so impressions arrive in bursts and cost per result runs higher than it needs to. {{voice_recommend}} broadening the targeting on the affected audiences, starting wider and narrowing later with demographic reporting, or consolidating several small audiences that share an objective so each campaign clears the recommended floor.

audit_checks.title: Too many attributes are layered, collapsing reach and durability
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Stacking many attributes onto one audience shrinks it and ties delivery to data that members update at their own pace. The audience narrows below a workable size and erodes as profiles go stale, so reach falls and results become harder to sustain.

educate.base:
LinkedIn lets an advertiser build an audience from professional attributes such as job title, seniority, job function, skills, and company, and these can be layered together so a member has to match every selected attribute to qualify. Each attribute added narrows the audience because the pool becomes the intersection of all of them rather than the union.

Layering many attributes onto one audience has two costs. The audience shrinks, often below a workable size for steady delivery. It also becomes fragile since every attribute depends on members keeping that part of their profile current. A member who changes roles but has not updated their title drops out of a title-targeted audience, while a job-function plus seniority audience still holds them. Targeting a job function plus a seniority is usually more durable than a long list of exact job titles, which members fill in inconsistently.

The audience panel lists every targeting facet in use, so you may want to read how many attributes are stacked and whether the same intent could be reached with fewer, broader ones. A few signs point to an over-layered audience:
  • Three or more attribute categories are combined with a long list inside one or more of them
  • The estimated size drops sharply each time another attribute is added
  • Exact job titles carry the targeting where a job function would cover the same roles
You may want to consider simplifying over-layered audiences to a job function or seniority that covers the intended roles, then using demographic reporting to confirm the audience is reaching the right members.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business (B2B) store, the buyer is often a specific role, which tempts a long stack of exact job titles. You may want to consider targeting the buyer's job function plus a seniority instead because titles for the same role vary by company and a function-based audience holds together as members move.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead generation campaigns often layer many attributes to reach a precise persona, which can shrink the audience below a workable size. You may want to confirm the persona can be reached with a job function and seniority rather than a long title list, so the audience stays large enough to deliver and durable as profiles change.

investigate.intro: Read the targeting facets on each active audience and confirm whether the same intent could be reached with fewer, broader attributes.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager, select the account, open 'Campaigns', and choose a campaign to confirm.
Open the campaign's audience setup and read the list of targeting facets in use, noting how many attribute categories are layered and how long each list inside them runs.
Watch the audience size estimate as you mentally remove an attribute, and record any audience that depends on a long exact-title list where a job function would cover the same roles.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active campaign, the targeting facets in use and whether the audience relies on a long exact-title list or a stack of three or more narrow attributes. An audience that reaches its intent with a job function or seniority and a small number of facets passes. An audience over-layered with many narrow attributes or a long title list is a fail recorded with the campaign name and the facets at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the targeting facets in use for each active campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more audiences on {{site_name}} stack many narrow attributes, which collapses their reach and ties delivery to profile fields members update at their own pace. As people change roles without updating exact titles, the audience erodes and reach falls. {{voice_recommend}} simplifying the affected audiences to a job function or seniority that covers the intended roles, replacing long exact-title lists where a broader facet would reach the same members, and confirming the result with demographic reporting.

audit_checks.title: Audience Expansion is left on, widening targeting beyond the chosen audience
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Audience Expansion delivers ads to members who resemble the selected audience rather than only the members the advertiser chose, and it is enabled by default. Budget then reaches people outside the intended targeting, which dilutes a precisely built audience and makes spend harder to attribute to the chosen attributes.

educate.base:
Audience Expansion is a LinkedIn setting that lets the delivery system show ads to members who resemble the chosen audience but do not match the selected attributes themselves. It is enabled by default on ad sets where it is available, per LinkedIn's Audience Expansion help article, so an audience built to precise attributes can deliver to look-alike members unless the setting is turned off.

The effect depends on intent. When reach matters more than precision, Audience Expansion can find additional qualified members. When the targeting was assembled deliberately to reach a narrow persona, the setting widens delivery past that persona and budget follows, so spend reaches members the advertiser did not select. The audience count shown during setup does not include expansion members, which makes the widened delivery easy to miss in reporting.

The setting is a single checkbox in the audience section of campaign setup, so you may want to read its state on each campaign and confirm it matches the intent behind the targeting. A few signs point to expansion working against a precise audience:
  • The Audience Expansion checkbox is selected on a campaign built to tightly defined attributes
  • Demographic reporting shows delivery to job titles or companies outside the selected targeting
  • The campaign reaches more members than the targeted audience size would suggest
You may want to consider clearing the Audience Expansion checkbox when precise targeting is the point, and adding any companies, titles, or seniority levels to exclude as exclusions so they stay out even when expansion is on.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business (B2B) store, Audience Expansion can deliver ads to members who resemble the buyer but sit outside the intended company size or industry, which spends budget on poor-fit prospects. You may want to confirm the setting matches the precision of the targeting and consider turning it off when the buyer pool is deliberately narrow.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead generation campaign, Audience Expansion can fill the form with leads from outside the targeted persona, raising volume while lowering fit. You may want to confirm whether the campaign is optimizing for volume or fit, and consider clearing the checkbox when lead quality against the chosen attributes is the goal.

investigate.intro: Read the Audience Expansion setting on each active campaign and confirm it matches the intent behind the targeting.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager, select the account, open 'Campaigns', and choose a campaign to confirm.
Open the campaign's audience setup and find the 'Enable Audience Expansion' checkbox in the audience section, noting whether it is selected.
Open the campaign's demographic reporting and check whether delivery is reaching job titles, functions, or companies outside the selected targeting, which points to expansion widening the audience.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active campaign, whether Audience Expansion is enabled and whether that setting matches the intent of the targeting. A campaign where the setting matches intent, expansion on for reach or off for precision, passes. A campaign built to precise attributes with Audience Expansion left on is a fail recorded with the campaign name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the Audience Expansion setting and the targeting intent for each active campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Audience Expansion is enabled on one or more precisely targeted campaigns on {{site_name}}. Because the setting is on by default, a carefully built audience grows to include look-alike members the advertiser did not select, and budget follows them past the intended targeting. {{voice_recommend}} clearing the Audience Expansion checkbox on campaigns where precise targeting is the goal, adding any companies, titles, or seniority levels to keep out as exclusions, and using demographic reporting to confirm delivery returns to the selected attributes.
